Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(mcp+cli): Round 3 evaluation improvements — 12 issues (#77)

* chore(main): release 0.1.24 (#76)

* nancy[ALP-816]: Sort by LOC descending everywhere LOC is shown in a list

- fmm_list_files: change default sort_by from "name" to "loc" (desc)
  - src/mcp/tools.rs: unwrap_or("loc") instead of unwrap_or("name")
  - src/cli/mod.rs: default_value = "loc" instead of "name"
  - src/mcp/schema.rs: update tool description and sort_by field description
  - Updated test list_files_default_sort_is_alphabetical_asc → list_files_default_sort_is_loc_desc
  - Updated pagination test to pass sort_by=name explicitly (files have equal LOC)

- fmm_file_outline: sort class methods by size descending (largest first)
  - src/format.rs line 49: replaced alphabetical sort with end-start descending
  - Added new test file_outline_methods_sorted_by_size_descending

- fmm_search filter results: sort FileSearchResult by loc descending
  - src/format.rs format_filter_search(): sort by b.loc.cmp(&a.loc) before formatting
  - Consistent with fmm_list_files — heaviest files surface first

All 457 tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* nancy[ALP-817]: fmm_list_files — add summary header (file count, total LOC, largest file)

- format_list_files() gains two new parameters: total_loc: usize and
  largest: Option<(&str, usize)> — both computed from the full filtered
  entry set before pagination, so the summary reflects the entire result
  even when only a page is shown

- New summary line emitted after directory (when present) and before total:
    summary: 1,662 files · 487,341 LOC · largest: injector.ts (1,109 LOC)

- Added format_count() helper: inserts comma thousands separators
  (e.g. 1234567 → "1,234,567")

- Both call sites updated:
  - src/mcp/tools.rs: tool_list_files() computes total_loc and largest
  - src/cli/navigate.rs: ls() computes total_loc and largest

- Tests:
  - format_count_inserts_commas: verifies thousands formatting
  - list_files_summary_header_included: verifies summary line, file count,
    total LOC, largest annotation, and row format unchanged

All 459 tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* nancy[ALP-818]: fmm_list_files — add directory rollup mode (group_by: subdir)

- New group_by parameter on fmm_list_files and fmm ls
- group_by=subdir collapses files into immediate-subdirectory buckets;
  each bucket shows directory path, file count, and total LOC
- sort_by applies to bucket-level LOC (default: loc desc, same as flat mode)

Implementation:
- compute_rollup_buckets() in format.rs: shared by MCP and CLI
  - strips the directory prefix from each path
  - takes the first path segment as the bucket key
  - files directly in prefix → prefix bucket; no prefix → "(root)"
- format_list_files_rollup() in format.rs: column-aligned bucket output
  with summary header (total files and LOC)
- build_rollup() in mcp/tools.rs: thin wrapper calling both
- ListFilesArgs gains group_by: Option<String>; MCP schema gains group_by enum
- CLI: --group-by subdir flag on fmm ls; main.rs dispatch updated

Tests added (mcp/tests.rs):
- list_files_group_by_subdir_buckets_files_by_immediate_dir
- list_files_group_by_invalid_returns_error

All 461 tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* nancy[ALP-819]: fmm_list_files — add source/test filter (filter: source | tests | all)

- New filter parameter on fmm_list_files and fmm ls --filter
- filter=source: excludes test-pattern files
- filter=tests: returns only test-pattern files
- filter=all (default): no change in behaviour

Test detection heuristic (configurable via .fmmrc.json test_patterns):
- path_contains: /e2e/, /test/, /tests/, /spec/, /__tests__/
- filename_suffixes: .spec.ts, .test.ts, .e2e-spec.ts, .spec.js, .test.js,
  _test.go, _test.rs, .spec.tsx, .test.tsx

Config changes (src/config/mod.rs):
- Added TestPatterns struct with path_contains + filename_suffixes fields
- Added test_patterns field to Config (defaults to the heuristic above)
- Added Config::is_test_file(path) method
- Test patterns fully override when specified in .fmmrc.json

MCP changes:
- ListFilesArgs gains filter: Option<String>
- tool_list_files loads Config from _root and applies filter before pagination
- MCP schema gains filter enum with description of heuristic

CLI changes:
- ls() gains filter: &str parameter; --filter flag on fmm ls
- Uses Config::load_from_dir from root returned by load_manifest()

Tests added (config/mod.rs):
- is_test_file_detects_spec_suffix
- is_test_file_detects_path_segment
- test_patterns_configurable_via_fmmrc

All 469 tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* nancy[ALP-820]: fmm_list_files — add sort_by=downstream + downstream column in output

- sort_by=downstream: ranks files by direct dependent count (blast-radius sort)
  - MCP: added "downstream" to sort_by enum and sort logic
  - CLI: --sort-by now accepts "downstream"
  - Default sort is still "loc"; downstream defaults to desc like loc/exports

- Downstream count now shown in file row output when > 0:
    - src/core/injector.ts  # loc: 1109, exports: 3, ↓ 47 downstream
  - format_list_files tuple changed from (&str, usize, usize) to
    (&str, usize, usize, usize) — (path, loc, exports, downstream_count)
  - Populated from manifest.reverse_deps at collection time (O(1) lookup)

- Schema updated:
  - sort_by enum gains "downstream" value
  - Tool description lists all 4 sort options explicitly
  - sort_by field description explains downstream as "blast-radius sort"

- exports sort was already working — now confirmed in schema description text

All 730 tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* review[ALP-815]: add filter tests + fix order schema description

- Add 3 MCP integration tests for filter=source, filter=tests, and
  filter=<invalid> (ALP-819 had config-level tests but no MCP plumbing
  tests; this covers the tool_list_files path end-to-end)
- Fix order field description in schema to include 'downstream' alongside
  'loc'/'exports' as a sort that defaults to descending

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* nancy[ALP-821]: fmm_list_files — add sort_by=modified + modified column in output

- Add `modified: Option<String>` field to `FileEntry` struct
- Parse `modified:` from sidecar YAML _extra map in `parse_sidecar()`
- `From<Metadata> for FileEntry` initialises `modified: None`
- Add `"modified"` to valid sort_by values in MCP tools, CLI, schema, and help text
- Sort is lexicographic on YYYY-MM-DD strings (correct for ISO dates); files without
  a modified date sort last
- Expand entries tuple to (\&str, usize, usize, usize, Option<\&str>) throughout
  tools.rs, navigate.rs, and format.rs
- `format_list_files` gains `show_modified: bool` param; when true, adds
  `modified: YYYY-MM-DD` annotation to each row (only shown for sort_by=modified)
- 4 new tests in mcp/tests.rs: desc default, asc order, date visible in output,
  composable with directory filter
- Updated all FileEntry literal initialisers in test code to include `modified: None`
- CLI: fmm ls --sort-by now accepts modified, added example to --help

* nancy[ALP-822]: fmm_read_symbol — redirect bare class reads to outline when 10KB cap would be hit

When fmm_read_symbol is called with a bare class name (no dot) and the
symbol source would exceed the 10KB response cap, return a method outline
with redirect hints instead of a misleading partial view of the class.

- Add `truncate: Option<bool>` to `ReadSymbolArgs` so the truncate flag
  flows into the tool function (previously only read in mod.rs cap_response)
- Make `McpServer::MAX_RESPONSE_BYTES` pub(crate) so tools.rs can reference it
  for the pre-response size check
- In `tool_read_symbol`: after computing symbol_source, check if:
    - bare name (no dot) AND truncate enabled (default true)
    - source.len() > MAX_RESPONSE_BYTES
    - file entry has methods for this class
  If all three: return `format_class_redirect` instead of the truncated source
- Add `format_class_redirect` to format.rs:
    - Header comment noting class size and method count
    - symbol/file/lines block
    - methods: section sorted by line order with line ranges
    - Redirect hints: dotted-notation example + truncate: false reminder
- Classes without methods (or under 10KB) pass through unchanged
- `truncate: false` bypasses the redirect and returns full source
- 3 integration tests in tests/mcp_tools.rs:
    - bare class over cap → redirect returned
    - truncate: false on large class → full source returned
    - small class → no redirect

* nancy[ALP-823]: fmm_search — fix AND semantics for multi-filter queries

Previously filter_search() built file_set using OR/union semantics: each
filter added its matching files to the set, with only the LOC filter using
retain(). The result was files matching export OR imports, filtered by LOC
— not the AND the caller expects.

Fix: invert the model.
- Start with all files in file_set
- Each active filter narrows with retain() (AND semantics)
- No-filters case short-circuits before the retain chain

export filter: build matching file path set (exact O(1) first, then fuzzy
substring fallback), retain only files in that set.

imports filter: retain files that import the given package/module name.

depends_on filter: retain files whose dependencies resolve to the target
path via dep_targets_file() or substring fallback, or whose imports match
via dotted_dep_matches().

LOC filter: retain files within the loc range (unchanged, already used retain).

5 new integration tests in tests/mcp_tools.rs:
- export AND imports (both required)
- export AND min_loc (min_loc excludes matching file)
- imports AND min_loc (file satisfies both)
- three-filter AND (imports + min_loc + export all match)
- three filters where one mismatches → empty result

* nancy[ALP-824]: fmm_list_exports — verify and test truncation notice when results exceed limit

The truncation notice was already present in format_list_exports_pattern()
via the '# showing: X-Y of N' and '# next: Use offset=N to continue.'
lines (added in an earlier iteration). The issue was filed from an
evaluation that predates this code being in place.

Added 2 tests to document and lock the behavior:
- list_exports_truncation_notice_shown_when_limit_reached: verifies
  '#showing:' / 'of N' / 'offset=' appear when limit < total
- list_exports_no_truncation_notice_when_all_fit: verifies no notice
  when all results fit within the requested limit

* nancy[ALP-825]: fmm_dependency_graph — annotate circular deps with # circular

In format_dependency_graph(): build a HashSet of local_deps paths and
append '  # circular' to any downstream entry that appears in both lists.

In format_dependency_graph_transitive(): same treatment — build a
HashSet from upstream paths, annotate matching downstream entries.

Zero overhead when no circular dependencies exist (HashSet construction
is skipped when downstream is empty, and set membership is O(1)).

Added 4 unit tests covering:
- no circular → output unchanged (both variants)
- circular entry annotated correctly (both variants)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* nancy[ALP-826]: fmm_glossary — contextualise empty and file-level results

Two UX improvements for fmm_glossary output:

1. Dotted query with zero call-site callers after refinement now returns a
   contextual message instead of a bare `used_by: []`:

     Injector.loadInstance:
       (no external source callers)
       # 1 file imports injector.ts — none call loadInstance directly
       # 1 test caller found (rerun with mode: tests)

   Logic: capture pre-refinement used_by counts before call_site_finder runs.
   If all sources empty after refinement, build the contextual YAML and return
   early. Test-caller hint only shown when mode=source and test importers exist
   (via new Manifest::count_test_dependents()).

2. Bare-name queries that match a dotted method_index entry now append a nudge:

     # Showing file-level importers (1 file imports injector.ts).
     # For call-site precision: pattern "Injector.loadInstance"

   Condition: pattern has no dot AND at least one result entry has a dotted
   name (i.e. came from method_index). No nudge when no dotted entries match.

Implementation:
- Manifest::count_test_dependents(file) — wraps find_dependents() + is_test_file filter
- tool_glossary() — pre-refinement snapshot, early-return path for Case 1, nudge
  append for Case 2

Added 5 integration tests in tests/glossary.rs covering both scenarios and
their negative cases.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* nancy[ALP-827+ALP-829]: private member access + line_numbers flag

ALP-827 — fmm_file_outline + fmm_read_symbol: include_private: true

  New module src/manifest/private_members.rs:
  - extract_private_members(root, file, class_names) → HashMap<class, Vec<PrivateMember>>
    Performs on-demand tree-sitter parse — no sidecar rebuild needed.
    TypeScript: private/protected methods + property declarations.
    Python: _underscore methods (excludes dunder __name__ methods).
    Other languages: graceful empty fallback.
  - find_private_method_range(root, file, class_name, method_name) → Option<(start, end)>
    Used by fmm_read_symbol to resolve private dotted names.

  fmm_file_outline(file, include_private: true):
  - New include_private: Option<bool> arg in FileOutlineArgs
  - format_file_outline() gains private_by_class: Option<&HashMap<...>> param
  - Private members shown under each class, annotated "# private"
  - Class header shows "N public methods, M private methods"
  - Public + private interleaved by line number when both present
  - All existing callers pass None (no behaviour change)

  fmm_read_symbol("ClassName._privateMethod"):
  - Dotted lookup now falls back to find_private_method_range when not in method_index
  - Error path now includes fmm_file_outline suggestion

  7 unit tests in private_members.rs (TS method, TS field, Python, etc.)
  Schema updated: fmm_file_outline + fmm_read_symbol descriptions + parameters.

  MISSING for next iteration: integration tests in tests/mcp_tools.rs,
  README + fmm skill docs update.

ALP-829 — fmm_read_symbol: line_numbers: true flag

  format_read_symbol() gains line_numbers: bool param.
  When true: splits source by newline, prepends right-aligned absolute line
  numbers (el.start + i), width = len(last_line_number).
  Default false — all existing callers unchanged.

  ReadSymbolArgs gains line_numbers: Option<bool>.
  tool_read_symbol passes args.line_numbers.unwrap_or(false).
  Schema updated: description + line_numbers property added.
  3 unit tests: flag=false unchanged, flag=true numbers correct, alignment.

  MISSING for next iteration: integration test in tests/mcp_tools.rs,
  README + fmm skill docs update.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* nancy[ALP-827+ALP-829]: README — fmm_file_outline + fmm_read_symbol updated

fmm_file_outline: note include_private: true for private member access.
fmm_read_symbol: note line_numbers: true and private method access.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* review[ALP-815]: fix private field annotation in mixed public+private outline

In format_file_outline(), the (false, _) branch (class has both public
methods and private members) used a bool to track is_private in the
combined interleaved list, then uniformly applied '  # private' to all
private entries.

This meant private *fields* got '  # private' instead of '  # private field',
inconsistent with the (true, _) branch which correctly distinguishes them.

Fix: change the combined tuple's last element from bool to &str (the
pre-computed suffix). Private methods get '  # private', private fields
get '  # private field', public methods get ''.

Added regression test covering a class with both public methods and a
private field to lock the correct annotation going forward.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: OAuth callback redirect loop — ensure session set before redirect

The callback's getAll() only returned request.cookies, so when
exchangeCodeForSession internally called getAll after setAll, it
couldn't see cookies it had just written (e.g. PKCE code_verifier).

Fix: mirror setAll writes back into request.cookies so subsequent
getAll calls within the same exchange see them. This matches the
pattern used in middleware.ts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Make submit a question banner prominent at top of FAQ page

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

overlay: downgrade Load rejection error log from WARN to DEBUG

Peers sending ERROR code=Load (peer rejected) is a routine load-balancing
event and fires constantly during peer churn. Keep other error codes at WARN.

commit.message:
Show paid-off completion state on debt cards

Debts with $0 balance now display a green border, checkmark icon,
strikethrough name, "Paid off!" badge, and "Debt demolished!" message.
Payment details and promo sections are hidden for paid-off debts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Graceful fallback when long-lived token exchange fails

The ig_exchange_token endpoint fails for non-test users when the app
hasn't completed Meta's App Review. Now falls back to the short-lived
token (~1 hour) so the signup flow still completes. Also reverts
token endpoints back to GET (the documented method).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix cross-org stale asset links surviving re-validation v3.13.75

The existing-link re-validation in _map_device_to_asset only checked
serial/hostname match but not org membership. After the org-assignment
fix (v3.13.63) moved devices to correct client orgs, old links to
assets in the core/wrong org were kept whenever hostname happened to
match — causing devices to display linked to unrelated assets.

Fix: validation now requires the linked asset to be in the same org
as the device AND have a matching serial number or hostname. Any link
that fails the org check is cleared and re-evaluated from scratch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix FX conversions: book expenses in destination currency with @@ rate

25 entries where bank did FX conversion now properly show:
- Bank debit in source currency (USD/EUR)
- Expense in destination currency (GBP/CHF/EUR) with @@ total cost

Affected: personal transfers (USD->GBP), HMRC (EUR->GBP), Flywire/education
(USD->GBP), Ediblesites loans (EUR->GBP), Rennie/Bigwood (USD->GBP),
Libertas/Prodigy-MT (EUR->CHF), Ireland Revenue refund (EUR->USD).

Tightened account currency constraints (removed USD from King-Alfred, Bigwood).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: hardcode nginx listen port to 80

Remove dependency on ${PORT} env var substitution. Railway may inject
PORT as a value that differs from the UI port setting (80), causing
nginx to listen on the wrong port and resulting in connection refused.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add favicon suite: 16/32/192/512 PNG variants, SVG, apple-touch-icon, and manifest

- Vercel will auto-detect favicon for project thumbnail

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

v0.7.0: differential fingerprint, bridged ring fix, whitepaper

- Add chirality_differential_fingerprint() using only diff_cos + diff_sin
  sub-vectors (16 raw dims -> 128). Enantiomer cosine: -0.09 to -0.93
  (vs full FP 0.72-0.98). Achiral norm = 0. Modes: pure_diff, chi_diff.
- Add _is_bridged_bicyclic() detection via SSSR ring overlap analysis.
  Bridged rings (norbornane, camphor, DXM) get dedicated fallback chain
  with MMFF at all stages for correct bridgehead geometry.
- Expose fingerprint + differential_fingerprint on ChiralityResult.
- 24 new tests (73 total in modified files), 296/302 full suite pass.
- 7-page whitepaper with actual benchmark numbers.
- Atlas v5 diff FP benchmark script.

commit.message:
feat(web): intercept file path links in agent messages to open workspace viewer (#305) (#305)

Agent messages (e.g. from Codex) often contain file path links like
`omnimem/core.py:4318` which browsers resolve as broken server URLs.

Now these links are intercepted and open the file in the workspace panel:
- Delegated click handler on MessageItem detects file path hrefs
- WorkspacePanel auto-selects the sending agent, switches to files tab
- File is requested via WebSocket and displayed with syntax highlighting
- Target line is highlighted and scrolled into view

commit.message:
Cluster lifecycle attestations with Graunde delivery (#657)

* Emit cluster lifecycle attestations and deferred news for Graunde

Cluster topology changes (births/deaths) now leave an attestation trail
so the knowledge graph captures how understanding evolved. Deferred news
summaries are project-scoped for Graunde to deliver on the next session
Stop — closing the loop between background Pulse work and developer
awareness.

Limitation: no ack-checking yet; subsequent runs overwrite undelivered
news. Graunde writes delivered:* acks but QNTX doesn't read them.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Route cluster labeling through HandlePromptDirect for OpenRouter support

The labeling handler called the provider factory directly, which only
resolves to local inference. The Prompt glyph already handles OpenRouter
via plugin forwarding in HandlePromptDirect — reuse that path so cluster
labeling works with whatever provider is configured.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Accumulate deferred news across runs until Graunde delivers

Before emitting cluster update news, check if Graunde has acked the
previous one. If not, prepend prior undelivered detail so no topology
changes are lost between sessions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove temporary debug log lines from deferred news emitters

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Extract cluster label system prompt to a constant

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Workspace cleanup + recover Copywriting builds from Shopify

- Remove 5,900+ dead files: VIVIA_OS screenshots, old shopify-theme/,
  shopify-backup-restore/, playwright_profile/, __pycache__, temp files,
  root-level one-off scripts (.py, .html, .csv, .json, .bat)

  Shopify liquid files (originals lost in case-sensitivity deletion)

fix: stats section not reading 'number' field from mock data

The mock homepage data uses 'number' for stat values but the component
only checked 'value' and 'statValue'. Added 'number' to the fallback chain.
